- Three underlying problems identified: missing territory definition (the "constitution"), bad recent data entry, and a genuine capacity gap on Mondays/Wednesdays (3 trucks vs. demand).
- Claude proposed a fixed territory-day model: drawn ZIP/area → technician + weekday assignments (declared by Spencer, not inferred), with customers getting permanent home slots and the optimizer demoted to sequencing/timing only.
- Three-phase implementation path outlined: Phase 1 (~1 hour) — generate proposed grid from 12-month history and have Spencer correct it; Phase 2 — one-time migration writing home slots to Jobber; Phase 3 — daily automation running on rails with overflow/violation flags.
- Claude recommended against writing to Jobber this weekend; instead, build the grid correctly during the calm week rather than force a bridge board that is "80% right and 20% arguable."
- Two options presented to Spencer: pause and build the system correctly, or bridge this week's operations while building the grid in parallel.
